buggy_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

prices = pd.Series(
    [100, 102, 101, 105, 103, 108],
    index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=6)
)

def local_zscore(window):
    # Only uses data within the window — no outer scope reference
    if window.std() == 0:
        return 0.0
    return (window[-1] - window.mean()) / window.std()

# raw=True passes numpy array for performance
zscore = prices.rolling(window=3).apply(local_zscore, raw=True)

signal = (zscore.shift(1) > 1.0).astype(int)
print(signal)
description: Clean example — rolling apply using only local window data with no outer scope references.
explanation: local_zscore uses only the window parameter. No closures over full-series variables. raw=True passes numpy array. Signal shifted by 1 before use.
